have h₁ : IntegrableOn (fun x : E => φ x • f x) (Ω) := by
    have h₂ : IntegrableOn f Ω μ := by
      have h₃ : LocallyIntegrableOn f Ω μ := hf

          -- Ω is a compact subset of itself
          exact isOpen_iff.mp (by exact Ω.isOpen)
          <;>
          simp_all [isCompact_iff_isClosed_and_bounded])
      exact h₄
    -- The function φ is smooth and has compact support, hence it is bounded
    have h₃ : BddBelow (Set.range φ) := by
      -- φ is a test function, so it is smooth and hence bounded
      have h₄ : Bdd (Set.range φ) := φ.bdd
      -- A bounded set is bounded below
      refine' (Set.bdd_iff_bddAbove_bddBelow.mp h₄).2
    have h₄ : BddAbove (Set.range φ) := by
      -- φ is a test function, so it is smooth and hence bounded

      -- A bounded set is bounded above
      refine' (Set.bdd_iff_bddAbove_bddBelow.mp h₅).1
    -- Since φ is bounded and f is integrable, their product is integrable

      -- Use the fact that φ is bounded and f is integrable
      have h₆ : ∃ (C : ℝ), ∀ x ∈ Set.univ, ‖φ x‖ ≤ C := by

      -- Use the bound to show that the product is integrable

      have h₈ : ∀ x ∈ Ω, ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ ‖φ x‖ * ‖f x‖ := by

          _ ≤ ‖φ x‖ * ‖f x‖ := by

      have h₉ : ∀ x ∈ Ω, ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ C * ‖f x‖ := by

                _ ≤ C := by
                  have h₁₃ : |φ x| ≤ C := by
                    have h₁₄ := hC x (Set.mem_univ x)
                    simp_all [Real.dist_eq, abs_le]
                    <;>
                    linarith
                  exact h₁₃
            exact h₁₂
          exact h₁₁
        have h₁₁ : ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ ‖φ x‖ * ‖f x‖ := h₈ x hx
        calc
          ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ ‖φ x‖ * ‖f x‖ := h₁₁
          _ ≤ C * ‖f x‖ := by gcongr <;> simp_all
      -- Use the bound to show that the product is integrable
      have h₁₀ : IntegrableOn (fun x : E => ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖) Ω := by
        refine' IntegrableOn.of_nonneg_of_le (fun x hx => by positivity) (fun x hx => _) h₂.abs
        -- Use the bound to show that the product is integrable
        have h₁₁ : ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ C * ‖f x‖ := h₉ x hx
        calc
          ‖(φ x • f x : F)‖ ≤ C * ‖f x‖ := h₁₁
          _ = C * ‖f x‖ := by rfl
      -- Use the bound to show that the product is integrable
      have h₁₁ : IntegrableOn (fun x : E => φ x • f x) Ω := by
        -- Use the bound to show that the product is integrable

            have h₁₂ : StronglyMeasurableOn (fun x : E => φ x • f x) Ω := by
              -- φ is a test function, so it is smooth and hence measurable

                -- φ is smooth, hence measurable

                -- f is locally integrable, so it is measurable
